description: An Italian pasta made of small filled pockets of dough, commonly stuffed with cheese, meat, seafood, or vegetables and served with a sauce such as tomato, butter and sage, or cream sauce.
preparation_steps: Prepare or roll out pasta dough.
Place small portions of filling on the dough and seal with another sheet or fold over.
Cut into individual ravioli and seal the edges well.
Boil until the pasta is tender and the filling is heated through.

nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, but higher with rich fillings or sauces

health_advice.benefits: Can provide energy and, depending on the filling, a useful amount of protein or vegetables.
health_advice.risks: Portion size, refined flour pasta, cheese-rich fillings, and creamy sauces can increase calories and saturated fat.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use a vegetable-based sauce or lighter butter sauce and pair with salad or vegetables.

dietary_notes.note: Traditional ravioli usually contains wheat and often egg. Vegan and gluten-free versions are possible with alternative dough and suitable filling.

allergens: Possible nuts in some fillings or sauces
